title: TJT 2026 배재고 고1 1학기 중간 본문
paragraphs: Lesson 1 Getting to Know Yourself: Don’t Let Anger Be Your Boss.
​Do you ever find yourself suddenly feeling angry?
One moment you’re fine, and the next moment you’re slamming doors or rolling your eyes at your parents.
Some people may think you just have a bad attitude or are getting too upset about a small problem.
However, these feelings don’t simply come from stress about everyday life.
Where does adolescent anger come from?
As a teen, you may feel like an adult, but your brain is still growing.
In your brain, there is a part that is responsible for solving problems and controlling your emotions.
In your teen years, this part is still in development.
The changes in your brain lead you toward independence, social engagement, and creativity.
At the same time, however, you may feel confused about who you are.
You may also be more sensitive to unfairness.
Furthermore, your body is flowing with hormones.
This significantly affects your mood.
The combination of these factors intensifies your strong emotions.
Getting to Know Your Anger.
​It is natural to feel angry from time to time; it doesn't mean there is something wrong with you.
Anger can sometimes be a useful tool.
For example, an angry but open conversation can resolve a conflict between friends.
The important thing is to manage your anger so that it does not turn into aggressive or violent behavior.
This does not mean that you should stop yourself from feeling angry.
Rather, you should express it in a productive way.
Ask yourself some questions about your angry feelings.
This can help you to better understand your anger.
​Questions to Ask Yourself.
How often do I feel angry?
What situations make me feel the most intense anger?
Is my anger directed at anyone or anything in particular?
Do I focus on the causes of my anger instead of solutions?
Can I control my emotions when I get angry?
How do I react and behave when I get angry?
First Aid to Calm Yourself Down.
​You need to learn how to deal with anger in a socially appropriate way.
Remember: anger is a feeling, but behavior is a choice.
The first step is recognizing the physical signs of anger.
It is possible to recognize those signs in advance.
They include an increased heart rate, a flushed face, and the clenching of your fists.
When these things happen, take action to reduce your anger.
For example, you could try taking a break to organize your thoughts or ending a conversation before it gets too intense.
Finding Proper Coping Skills.
​In some cases, the strategies mentioned above may not be enough, so you may need to find proper coping skills for your personal situation.
Without the right coping skills, you may find yourself becoming verbally or even physically aggressive.
Explore various strategies to discover what is best for you, such as taking a walk, drawing a picture, or writing down things that come to your mind.
You can also try to find possible solutions to a problem and then compare the advantages and disadvantages of each one.
By developing problem-solving skills, you will learn that there are many ways to solve a problem without getting angry.
Anger is an emotion that we all experience.
By managing your anger properly, you can be the boss of your feelings, not the other way around.
This is an important step in finding your true self and taking control of your life.
author: 이하준

sentences: Lesson 1 Don’t Let Anger Be Your Boss.
Do you ever find yourself suddenly feeling angry?
One moment you’re fine, and the next moment you’re slamming doors or rolling your eyes at your parents.
Some people may think you just have a bad attitude or are getting too upset about a small problem.
However, these feelings don’t simply come from stress about everyday life.
meanings: 레슨 1 화가 당신을 다스리게 하지 마세요
당신은 갑자기 화가 나는 경우가 있는가?
한 순간 당신은 괜찮다가 다음 순간에 문을 꽝 닫거나 부모님께 눈을 치켜뜬다.
어떤 사람들은 당신이 그저 태도가 나쁘거나 작은 문제에 너무 화를 낸다고 생각할지도 모른다.
그러나 이러한 감정들은 단순히 일상생활에 대한 스트레스에서 오는 것이 아니다.

sentences: Where does adolescent anger come from?
As a teen, you may feel like an adult, but your brain is still growing.
In your brain, there is a part that is responsible for solving problems and controlling your emotions.
In your teen years, this part is still in development.
The changes in your brain lead you toward independence, social engagement, and creativity.
At the same time, however, you may feel confused about who you are.
You may also be more sensitive to unfairness.
Furthermore, your body is flowing with hormones. This significantly affects your mood.
The combination of these factors intensifies your strong emotions.
meanings: 청소년의 분노는 어디에서 오는 걸까?
십대로서, 당신은 어른인 것처럼 느낄지도 모르지만 당신의 뇌는 여전히 자라고 있다.
당신의 뇌에는 문제 해결과 감정 조절을 담당하는 부분이 있다.
당신의 십대 시절에 이 부분은 여전히 발달하고 있다.
당신의 뇌의 변화는 당신을 독립성, 사회적 참여, 그리고 창의성으로 이끈다.
그러나 동시에 당신은 당신이 누구인지에 대해 혼란스럽게 느낄지도 모른다.
당신은 또한 불공정에 더욱 민감할 수도 있다.
게다가, 당신의 신체는 호르몬으로 넘쳐나고 있다. 이것은 당신의 기분에 상당히 영향을 미친다.
이러한 요인들의 조합은 당신의 강한 감정을 강화한다.

sentences: Getting to Know Your Anger.
It is natural to feel angry from time to time; it doesn’t mean there is something wrong with you.
Anger can sometimes be a useful tool.
For example, an angry but open conversation can resolve a conflict between friends.
The important thing is to manage your anger so that it does not turn into aggressive or violent behavior.
This does not mean that you should stop yourself from feeling angry.
Rather, you should express it in a productive way.
Ask yourself some questions about your angry feelings.
This can help you to better understand your anger.
meanings: 당신의 화를 알아보기
때때로 화가 나는 것은 자연스러우며 그것은 당신에게 문제가 있다는 것을 뜻하지 않는다.
화는 가끔 쓸모 있는 도구가 될 수 있다.
예를 들어, 격앙되지만 진솔한 대화는 친구들 사이의 갈등을 해결할 수 있다.
중요한 것은 당신의 화가 공격적이거나 폭력적인 행동으로 변하지 않도록 조절하는 것이다.
이는 당신이 화를 내지 말아야 한다는 것을 의미하지 않는다.
오히려 당신은 화를 생산적인 방식으로 표현해야 한다.
당신의 성난 감정에 대해 스스로에게 몇 가지 질문을 해 봐라.
이는 당신의 화를 더 잘 이해하는 데 도움이 될 수 있다.

sentences: Questions to Ask Yourself.
How often do I feel angry?
What situations make me feel the most intense anger?
Is my anger directed at anyone or anything in particular?
Do I focus on the causes of my anger instead of solutions?
Can I control my emotions when I get angry?
How do I react and behave when I get angry?
meanings: 스스로에게 물어볼 질문
나는 얼마나 자주 화가 나는가?
어떤 상황이 나에게 가장 극심한 화를 느끼게 하는가?
나의 화는 특히 어떤 사람 혹은 어떤 것을 향해 있는가?
나는 해결책보다는 내 화의 원인에 초점을 두는가?
화가 났을 때 나는 내 감정을 조절할 수 있는가?
화가 났을 때 나는 어떻게 반응하고 행동하는가?

sentences: First Aid to Calm Yourself Down.
You need to learn how to deal with anger in a socially appropriate way.
Remember: anger is a feeling, but behavior is a choice.
The first step is recognizing the physical signs of anger.
It is possible to recognize those signs in advance.
They include an increased heart rate, a flushed face, and the clenching of your fists.
When these things happen, take action to reduce your anger.
For example, you could try taking a break to organize your thoughts or ending a conversation before it gets too intense.
meanings: 스스로를 진정시키기 위한 응급 처치
당신은 사회적으로 적절한 방식으로 화를 다스리는 방법을 배워야 한다.
기억하라, 화는 감정이지만 행동은 선택이다.
첫 번째 단계는 화의 신체적 징후를 알아차리는 것이다.
그러한 징후를 미리 알아차리는 것이 가능하다.
그것들은 증가한 심박수, 빨개진 얼굴, 그리고 주먹을 꽉 쥐는 것을 포함한다.
이러한 것들이 발생할 때 당신의 화를 줄이기 위해 조치를 취해라.
예를 들어, 당신의 생각을 정리하기 위해 휴식을 취하거나 대화가 너무 격해지기 전에 중단하는 것을 시도해 볼 수 있다.

sentences: Finding Proper Coping Skills.
어떤 경우에는 위에 언급한 전략이 충분하지 않을 수 있으므로 당신의 개인 상황에 대한 적절한 대처 기술을 찾아야 할지도 모른다.
올바른 대처 기술이 없다면 당신은 스스로가 언어적으로 혹은 심지어 신체적으로 공격적이 되는 것을 발견할지도 모른다.
무엇이 당신에게 가장 좋은지 찾기 위해 산책하기, 그림 그리기, 혹은 마음에 떠오르는 것들을 적기와 같은 다양한 전략을 탐구해라.
당신은 또한 문제에 대한 가능한 해결책을 찾고 각각의 장점과 단점을 비교하려고 노력할 수 있다.
문제 해결 능력을 발전시킴으로써 당신은 화를 내지 않고 문제를 해결할 많은 방법이 있다는 것을 알게 될 것이다.
meanings: In some cases, the strategies mentioned above may not be enough, so you may need to find proper coping skills for your personal situation.
Without the right coping skills, you may find yourself becoming verbally or even physically aggressive.
Explore various strategies to discover what is best for you, such as taking a walk, drawing a picture, or writing down things that come to your mind.
You can also try to find possible solutions to a problem and then compare the advantages and disadvantages of each one.
By developing problem-solving skills, you will learn that there are many ways to solve a problem without getting angry.

sentences: Anger is an emotion that we all experience.
By managing your anger properly, you can be the boss of your feelings, not the other way around.
This is an important step in finding your true self and taking control of your life.
meanings: 화는 우리 모두가 겪는 감정이다.
화를 적절하게 다룸으로써 당신은 그 반대가 아니라 당신이 감정의 주도권을 가질 수 있다.
이것은 진정한 자아를 찾고 당신의 삶을 장악하는 데 있어 중요한 단계이다.
